questions.q: By what earlier name was August Kranti Maidan known?
questions.explanation: August Kranti Maidan was formerly known as Gowalia Tank Maidan.

questions.q: In which city is August Kranti Maidan located?
questions.explanation: The ground is located in Bombay, present-day Mumbai.

questions.q: On what date did Gandhi deliver the Quit India call at this ground?
questions.explanation: Gandhi delivered the Quit India call here on 8 August 1942.

questions.q: Which movement was launched from this ground in 1942?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched from this ground in August 1942.

questions.q: Why was the ground renamed August Kranti Maidan?
options: To mark the launch of the Quit India Movement
questions.explanation: It was renamed to commemorate the launch of the August 1942 Quit India Movement.

questions.q: What does 'Kranti' in August Kranti Maidan mean?
questions.explanation: 'Kranti' means revolution, referring to the August 1942 uprising.

questions.q: Which leader gave the 'Do or Die' call at this maidan?
questions.explanation: Gandhi gave the 'Do or Die' call here during the Quit India launch.

questions.q: In which Indian state is August Kranti Maidan today?
questions.explanation: The maidan is in Mumbai, in the state of Maharashtra.

questions.q: The ground is associated most directly with which month of nationalist activity?
questions.explanation: It is associated with the August 1942 launch of the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: What kind of place is August Kranti Maidan?
questions.explanation: It is an open public ground in Mumbai used for the historic 1942 gathering.

questions.q: Which body passed the Quit India resolution at this ground in 1942?
questions.explanation: The All India Congress Committee passed the Quit India resolution at Gowalia Tank Maidan.

questions.q: Which woman leader is remembered for hoisting the national flag at this ground in 1942?
questions.explanation: Aruna Asaf Ali hoisted the Congress flag at Gowalia Tank during the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: The original name 'Gowalia Tank' referred to what feature once near the ground?
questions.explanation: Gowalia Tank took its name from a water tank or pond that once existed in the area.

questions.q: The day after the resolution at this ground, what did the British do to Congress leaders?
questions.explanation: On 9 August 1942 the British arrested the Congress leadership in early-morning raids.

questions.q: Which famous two-word command associated with this ground became the watchword of the movement?
questions.explanation: Gandhi's 'Do or Die' exhortation at the ground became the movement's watchword.

questions.q: Why is the ground sometimes called the 'cradle' of the Quit India Movement?
options: It was where independence was declared in 1947
It is where Gandhi was born
The movement was formally launched there
questions.explanation: Because the Quit India Movement was launched there in August 1942, it is called the movement's cradle.

questions.q: In which present-day Mumbai locality does August Kranti Maidan lie?
questions.explanation: The maidan lies in the Grant Road and Tardeo area of south Mumbai.

questions.q: Who was Congress president and present at the time the resolution was adopted at this ground?
questions.explanation: Maulana Azad was Congress president when the Quit India resolution was adopted in 1942.

questions.q: What was the immediate British response to the events at this ground in August 1942?
options: A nationwide crackdown and mass arrests
questions.explanation: The British responded with a sweeping crackdown and the arrest of the Congress leadership.

questions.q: The renaming of the ground reflects which Marathi-Hindi term for the 1942 events?
questions.explanation: The events of August 1942 came to be called August Kranti, the August Revolution.

questions.q: The ground is most associated with which phase of the freedom struggle?
options: The final phase during the Second World War
questions.explanation: It is tied to the Quit India Movement of 1942, in the final wartime phase of the struggle.

questions.q: What commemorative feature now marks the historic site?
options: A memorial pillar and grounds honouring the 1942 movement
questions.explanation: The site is preserved with memorials commemorating the launch of the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: On what precise date did Gandhi deliver the lengthy 'Do or Die' speech at Gowalia Tank?
questions.explanation: Gandhi delivered the speech on the evening of 8 August 1942, after the resolution was passed.

questions.q: Aruna Asaf Ali's flag-hoisting at the ground occurred on which date in 1942?
questions.explanation: Aruna Asaf Ali hoisted the flag at Gowalia Tank on 9 August 1942 as leaders were being arrested.

questions.q: Who was the Viceroy of India when the Quit India resolution was adopted at this ground?
questions.explanation: Lord Linlithgow was Viceroy when the resolution was passed at Gowalia Tank in August 1942.

questions.q: In which exact words did Gandhi describe the mantra he gave the people at the ground?
options: A short one of 'Do or Die'
A call to seize the railways
A demand for a Constituent Assembly
questions.explanation: Gandhi said he would give them a short mantra: 'Do or Die', to free India or perish in the attempt.

questions.q: Which premier train running from Mumbai shares its name with the 1942 events commemorated by the maidan?
questions.explanation: The August Kranti Rajdhani Express, running from Mumbai to Delhi, draws its name from the same 1942 August Revolution commemorated by the maidan.

questions.q: Before becoming a focus of 1942, Gowalia Tank Maidan had hosted earlier Congress activity, reflecting its role as what kind of space?
options: A British military parade ground only
A traditional venue for political meetings in Bombay
questions.explanation: The maidan had long served as a public gathering space for political meetings in colonial Bombay.

questions.q: The slogan coined by Yusuf Meherally that defined the resolution at this ground was what?
questions.explanation: Yusuf Meherally coined 'Quit India', the phrase that named the movement launched at the ground.

questions.q: What broader symbolic meaning does August Kranti Maidan hold in India's freedom history?
options: It marks the start of the 1857 revolt
It symbolizes the final mass demand for British withdrawal
It commemorates the signing of the Constitution
It represents the partition of Bengal
questions.explanation: The ground stands as a symbol of the final, decisive mass demand for the British to quit India.
